Avenue Christian church Saturday
afternoon at 2 o'clock Rev. J. w.
Allen will officiate and the inter-
ment will be at Paramount GENE
terry.


Tea will be served at the Coun-
try club everp Sunday afternoon,
commencing February 10. This
was decided on by the house COM
mittee in view of the number of
members who spend the afternoon
at the club. Tea will be poured
by Miss Mabel WEIGH next Sunday.
The regular weekly dance will be
held tomorrow night.


An old stocking might have been
good bank for your ancestors,
but it is not safe place for you
to keep your idle money.


If you don't want to open an &C.
count you can deposit your money
with us on a certificate of deposit.


Liberal terms and interest.


Open Saturday evenings between
and p. m. -.


Spokane G. Eastern Trust Co.
Ida Settlers, 20 years of age, was
convicted of firing the home of w.
j. Maxwell, corner of Sixth ave-
nue. and Adams street, on the after
noon of Jan. 4, by jury sitting I'M
der Judge Poindexter in the super
ior court last night. The jury was
out four hours, the first ballot be-
ing 10 for conviction. The penalty
for arson is from five TO 10 years
Imprisonment and a pecuniary fine
at the discretion of the court.


l.. M. Davenport has decided to
the Spokane & Eastern Trust COM
pany the three story building adj
joining the trust company building
on the west at Sprague avenue and
Howard street. The building is ii
IL feet wide and the lower floor
of the building is occupied by a sa-
loon. 1t will be used by the Spo-
kane and Eastern Trust company
as soon as the lease of the present
tenant expires. The price of the
sale was not made public.


The Great Northern railway
company has filed its application
for a franchise to build viaducts
over in the eastern part of the city
over Regal street, Ada street, Bald
win avenue, North Center street,
Harrison avenue, Indiana avenue,
Nora avenue, Mission avenue,
Boone avenue, Superior street.
Hamilton street and Cincinnati
street. There Is to be a Is foot
clearance over each street. All
streets and avenues are to be left
at their natural grade and the
railroad company is to bear all ex-
pense.
